Package de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.op

Class ProgramVariable

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.op.AbstractSortedOperator
de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.op.ProgramVariable
All Implemented Interfaces:
Expression, ModelElement, ProgramElement, ReferencePrefix, ReferenceSuffix, SourceElement, TerminalProgramElement, Named, IProgramVariable, Operator, ParsableVariable, SortedOperator, SVSubstitute, ProgramInLogic, Sorted, EqualsModProofIrrelevancy
Direct Known Subclasses:
LocationVariable, ProgramConstant
public abstract class ProgramVariable extends AbstractSortedOperator implements SourceElement, ProgramElement, Expression, ReferencePrefix, IProgramVariable, ParsableVariable, ReferenceSuffix, ProgramInLogic
The objects of this class represent program variables and program constants (resulting from static final declarations in programs; TODO: it is weird that constants are a special case of variables). Additionally, as a legacy of the past, the RecodeR front end of KeY still creates objects of this class also for fields (aka. attributes, member variables), even though theoretically, these are *not* program variables (not any more)! Such fake "program variables" can be recognized by the fact that calling isMember() on them returns true. Thus, they must never occur in actual proofs. The method HeapLDT.getFieldSymbolForPV() serves to convert such fake program variables to the appropriate constant symbols.

    • getStartPosition

      public Position getStartPosition()
      Description copied from interface: SourceElement
      Returns the start position of the primary token of this element. To get the start position of the syntactical first token, call the corresponding method of getFirstElement().

      getStartPosition in interface SourceElement
      Returns:
      the start position of the primary token.

    • equalsModRenaming

      public boolean equalsModRenaming(SourceElement se, NameAbstractionTable nat)
      equals modulo renaming is described in the corresponding comment in class SourceElement. In this case two programvariables are considered to be equal if they are assigned to the same abstract name or if they are the same object.
      Specified by:
      equalsModRenaming in interface SourceElement

      public Expression convertToProgram(Term t, ExtList l)

      convertToProgram in interface ProgramInLogic

    • match

      public MatchConditions match(SourceData source, MatchConditions matchCond)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramElement
      matches the source "text" (@link SourceData#getSource()) against the pattern represented by this object. In case of a successful match the resulting MatchConditions with the found instantiations of the schemavariables. If the match failed, null is returned instead.
      Specified by:
      match in interface ProgramElement
      Parameters:
      source - the SourceData with the program element to match
      matchCond - the MatchConditions found up to this point
      Returns:
      the resulting match conditions or null if the match failed

    • validTopLevelException

      public void validTopLevelException(Term term) throws TermCreationException
      Description copied from interface: Operator
      Checks whether the top level structure of the given @link Term is syntactically valid, given the assumption that the top level operator of the term is the same as this Operator. The assumption that the top level operator and the term are equal is NOT checked.
      Specified by:
      validTopLevelException in interface Operator
      Throws:
      TermCreationException - if a construction error was recognise
